description_long: This delivery robot features a robust, boxy body with a predominantly orange and black color scheme, designed for urban navigation. Its head is a rectangular unit with a dark, likely transparent front panel, possibly housing sensors or a display. The robot is equipped with four large, rugged wheels, each with deep treads for traction on various surfaces, and the wheels are mounted on articulated axles for stability. The body includes multiple panels, buttons, and what appear to be access hatches, with some areas marked by warning labels and ports. The rear and sides show additional technical details, such as vents, ports, and possibly charging or maintenance access points. The overall design suggests durability and functionality for autonomous delivery tasks in city environments.
description: A rugged, orange and black delivery robot with a boxy body, four large treaded wheels, and multiple access panels and sensors, built for urban navigation.
description_view_0: Front and side view showing the robot's head with a dark panel, front buttons, and articulated wheels.
description_view_1: Rear and side view highlighting the back panel with warning labels, ports, and the robust wheel configuration.
description_view_2: Front and opposite side view displaying the head's sensor area, side access panels, and wheel details.
description_view_3: Full rear view showing the main access panel, warning insignia, and technical ports on the back.
attribute_wheel_configuration: Four large, rugged drive wheels with deep treads, arranged in pairs on each side for stability and traction.
attribute_compartment_access_panel: A rectangular access panel is visible on the rear of the body, featuring a handle and possible locking mechanism.
attribute_display_screen: A dark, rectangular display screen or sensor panel is present on the front of the head unit; it appears to be powered off with no visible content.
